text: IDF confirmed ground operation in southern Lebanon with approximately 100,000 troops across 6 divisions pushing south of the Litani River. 886 killed, 1 million displaced.
verificationNote: IDF official statement confirmed troop deployment

text: Iranian drone struck a fuel storage tank near Dubai International Airport, suspending flights for over 7 hours. Fujairah oil hub also struck again — oil loading operations suspended.
verificationNote: Dubai Airports and Fujairah port authority confirmed

text: IDF destroyed Khamenei's personal aircraft at Mehrabad Airport in Tehran. Part of expanded targeting of Iranian leadership assets and symbols of power.
IRGC declared the USS Gerald Ford carrier strike group's entire support network a legitimate military target, warning of potential attacks on supply and logistics vessels.
verificationNote: IRGC official statement via Tasnim News

text: Researchers identified 62 IRGC-linked social media accounts on X, Bluesky, and Instagram spreading coordinated disinformation narratives about civilian casualties and US involvement.
verificationNote: Clemson University Media Forensics Hub report

text: IDF 91st Division ground forces took control of Khiam in southern Lebanon, the largest town captured so far. Hezbollah forces reported withdrawing north of the Litani River.

Iran has selectively allowed passage through the Strait of Hormuz for ships from Pakistan, India, and Turkey, while Western-flagged commercial vessels remain blocked. Fewer than 5 ships per day transiting vs. 138 historical average.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera shipping investigation confirmed by maritime data

text: UAE closed its entire national airspace with all flights at Dubai International suspended. This is the world's busiest international airport handling 90+ million passengers annually.
verificationNote: UAE aviation authority and flight tracking data confirmed

text: Senior IRGC Quds Force commander Brig. Gen. Hossein al-Askari killed in drone strike on Baghdad's Green Zone. Was coordinating militia operations across Iraq — a major escalation of targeted killings.
verificationNote: Multiple Iraqi sources and IRGC media confirm

text: Oil dropped sharply — Brent fell 4.3% to $100.21 and WTI dropped 6.1% to $93.50 after Treasury Secretary Bessent said the US will allow Iranian tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. WSJ reports the US is building a multinational naval escort coalition.

Iran has sent at least 11.7 million barrels of crude through the Strait of Hormuz to China since the war began. Shadow fleet vessels continue operating despite the blockade that has halted all Western commercial shipping.

White House AI Czar David Sacks publicly called for finding an off-ramp, saying this is a good time to declare victory and exit. Warned that Iran has a 'dead man's switch' over Gulf states' economic fate, including desalination plants.
verificationNote: First senior Trump adviser to publicly break with war policy

text: IDF announced it killed Basij commander Gholamreza Soleimani, his deputy, and IRGC Aerospace Force chief in overnight strikes. Described as the biggest leadership decapitation night of the war so far.
verificationNote: Based on IDF announcement; awaiting Iran confirmation

text: UAE defense ministry reported 304 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, and 1,627 UAVs intercepted since the conflict began — a staggering number of Iranian projectiles aimed at Gulf states.
